title: Limb Injuries
content_markdown:
#### Management

- Haemorrhage control.
- Analgesia as per CMG 2 Pain Management.
- Check arterial circulation in the limb 
- If distal pulse is absent, gently re‐align fractured segments until pulse returns or alignment is near normal.

- In the absence of life‐threatening haemorrhage, open fractures or wounds should be irrigated prior to splinting.
- Immobilise/ splint all fractures unless the patient is otherwise time‐critical 
- Traction splint for suspected fractured mid‐shaft femur
- Pelvic splint for suspected pelvic fracture.
- Elevate the limb, if possible.
- Do not attempt to reduce dislocations.
- IV fluid as per CMG 14 Shock and Hypoperfusion.
- Crush injured limbs should be treated as per CMG 30 Crush Syndrome.

#### Partially Severed Limb

- Carefully protect limb.
- Keep distal portion of limb dry, wrapped and cool.

#### Completely Severed Limb

- Keep severed part dry, wrapped and cold 
- Place in a dry, sealed plastic bag
- Then place within another bag or bucket filled with iced water. 

- Do not immerse the part directly in ice.
- Do not attempt to clean or disinfect the severed part.
